persona.the_one_thing: She literally teaches enterprises how to adopt Claude, and her thesis is 'an AI pilot is a product launch, not an IT trial' (s5) plus 'clear thinking beats coding' (s3) — so walk in framing Orbt's Claude adoption as a workflow-change product launch and demonstrate prompting rigor, not as a procurement evaluation or a request for basics. Confidence: high (multiple about_target talk transcripts/reports).
persona.snapshot: Hannah Moran is on Anthropic's Applied AI team (since ~March 2025, per s10), where she is one of the public faces teaching how to actually build with and deploy Claude — co-presenting the 'Prompting 101' / 'Prompting for Agents' sessions at Code w/ Claude and contributing to Anthropic's context-engineering guidance (s6, s7, s30, s14). She came to AI through consulting rather than pure engineering: prior roles span PwC's Innovation Hub (AI & Emerging Tech), PwC Labs (ML data science), and PwC Digital, on top of a Georgetown degree in International Political Economy / International Business Diplomacy (s10, s1). Her recurring message is that structured, clear thinking — not coding fluency — is what makes AI work in the enterprise (s3).
persona.whats_new: Over the last ~12 months she has become a recognizable public teacher of prompting and agents at Anthropic: her 'agents are models using tools in a loop' line from the May 2025 workshop circulated widely (s2, s9, s12), her 'Prompting 101' talk with Christian Ryan is still being written up into 2026 (s7, dated May 2026), and her enterprise-pilot framing ('product launch, not IT trial') landed in Sep 2025 deployment coverage (s5, s8).

Listed as a contributor to Anthropic's 'Effective context engineering for AI agents' write-up; celebrated it on her own LinkedIn

top_moves.fact: She co-leads Anthropic's 'Prompting 101' and contributed to its context-engineering guidance, walking audiences through evolving a real customer prompt over five versions (s7, s14).
top_moves.inference: She judges counterparts on whether they actually understand structured, iterative prompting rather than on AI buzzwords.
top_moves.move: Bring one concrete prompt-iteration artifact from Orbt's agents (a before/after across versions, like her insurance-form demo) instead of a capabilities pitch — show your work.

top_moves.fact: She says an AI pilot is 'a product launch, not an IT trial' and 'don't teach too much of the basics... help them do their job differently, and better' (s5, s8).
top_moves.inference: She cares about changed workflows and outcomes, not feature tours or procurement checklists.
top_moves.move: Frame Orbt's Claude adoption as a workflow transformation for your consulting agents — name the job that gets done differently/better and the success metric, not the feature list.

top_moves.fact: Her crisp definition 'agents are models using tools in a loop' became a widely-shared reference point (s2, s9, s12).
top_moves.inference: She thinks about agents in clean architectural terms and will respond to precise language.
top_moves.move: Describe Orbt's Slack-delivered agents in her own framing — 'models using tools in a loop' running country strategy/revenue/creative — to speak her language directly.

non_obvious.insight: Her authority on prompting is built on a non-engineering foundation: a Georgetown degree in International Political Economy / International Business Diplomacy and a consulting path through PwC (Innovation Hub, Labs, Digital) — not a CS pipeline (s1, s10). That background is exactly why her public message is 'clear thinking beats coding' (s3); she is living proof of the thesis, which is why she leads with structure-of-language over code.

interaction_simulation.opening_30s: He opens with orbt's agents-for-cross-border-GTM and how the intelligence engine tests decisions without search; she immediately maps it onto 'models using tools in a loop' and asks how the loop is bounded.
interaction_simulation.likely_arc: Fast convergence on agent design and context engineering; she probes deployment realism (do Korean F&B/cosmetics operators actually change behavior?), he answers with architecture and proof points like Meet the Drapers top-4.
interaction_simulation.where_it_could_stall: If talk drifts to audience-facing framing/positioning, he may go quiet and want to return to the mechanism; if she stays at the adoption-narrative layer too long it can feel thin to him.
interaction_simulation.how_to_adapt: Bring a specific eval or failure mode of orbt's agents and ask how she'd structure the prompt/context for it — turns her teaching instinct into collaborative debugging, his preferred register.
